message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>The Coronavirus Disease 19 epidemic is an infectious disease which was declared as a pandemic and hit all the Countries, all over the world, from the beginning of the year 2020.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>Despite the emergency vigilance plans, in all the Countries, Health Systems experienced a different ratio of lethality, admissions to intensive care units and managing quarantine of positive patients.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>The aim of this study is to investigate if some health indicators might have been useful to understand the capacity of Italian National Health Service to manage the COVID 19 epidemic.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>We will compare data in two different Italian regions in the Northern part of Italy (Lombardy and Veneto) with the national data to understand if different health strategies might be significant to explain different patterns of COVID 19 epidemic in Italy.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>The two regions have two different health policies to face CoViD-2019 epidemic.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>To face epidemic like this one the answer should be outside hospitals but this means to have general practitioners well-trained and enough healthcare personnel working outside hospitals.</jats:p>
title: Are health indicators able to describe the ability to cope of Health Systems with COVID-19 epidemic?
